name.es: Pan de campo con masa madre y nueces (estilo Tartine)
name.fr: Pain rustique au levain et aux noix (style Tartine)

description.en: Chad Robertson's country loaf studded with whole toasted walnuts, which turn a startling deep purple as the long ferment works on their tannins. Rich and slightly bittersweet, substantial enough to anchor a cheese board or a bowl of soup.
description.de: Chad Robertsons Country-Brot, gespickt mit ganzen gerösteten Walnüssen, die durch die lange Gärung ein überraschend tiefes Violett annehmen. Reichhaltig, leicht bittersüß und kräftig genug für eine Käseplatte oder einen Teller Suppe.
description.es: El pan de campo de Chad Robertson salpicado de nueces enteras tostadas, que adquieren un sorprendente tono violeta oscuro durante la larga fermentación. Rico, ligeramente agridulce y contundente, perfecto junto a una tabla de quesos o un plato de sopa.
description.fr: Le pain rustique de Chad Robertson parsemé de noix entières torréfiées, qui prennent une étonnante teinte violette au fil de la longue fermentation. Riche, légèrement amer-doux et assez consistant pour accompagner un plateau de fromages ou une soupe.

origin.note: A Tartine Bakery variation on Chad Robertson's country loaf; distinct from the traditional French levain aux noix it takes inspiration from.

ingredients.note: mature, to build the overnight leaven

keeping.note: The crumb around the walnuts deepens to purple-brown over the first day, a normal reaction with fermentation tannins.

method.title: Toast the walnuts
method.summary: Toast shelled walnuts until darkened and fragrant, about 8-10 minutes at 200°C; cool completely.

method.title: Build the leaven
method.summary: The night before, mix starter with whole wheat flour and water; ferment overnight at room temperature.

method.title: Mix and autolyse
method.summary: Dissolve the leaven in most of the water, mix in both flours, and rest about 40 minutes.

method.title: Add salt, then bulk ferment
method.summary: Work in salt and reserved water, then give the dough six stretch-and-fold turns at 30-minute intervals, folding in the toasted walnuts partway through; leave undisturbed afterward for about 3 hours.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Divide in two, preshape, bench-rest 40 minutes, then shape one loaf as a batard and one as a boule.

method.title: Cold proof
method.summary: Retard overnight in the refrigerator, 16-18 hours.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake in a preheated Dutch oven at 230°C: about 25 minutes covered, then 35 minutes uncovered until deep brown.

method.title: Cool
method.summary: Rest at least an hour before slicing to reveal the walnut-flecked crumb.

source.author: Maurizio Leo, after Chad Robertson / Tartine Bread
source.adaptation: Percentages include the leaven's flour/water (only part of the build is used, prorated); walnut weight estimated from the stated '2 cups'.
